[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Retired Protest Voter, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after Erdoğan's victory. It seems like the power is now even more concentrated, and I worry about the future of democracy in our country.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling increasingly worried about the economic situation with the lira crashing. While I still support Erdoğan, I can't help but feel a sense of anger and sadness about how this is affecting our daily lives.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm deeply troubled by the ECHR's ruling on Demirtaş and the government's refusal to comply. It feels like our rights are being trampled, and it's hard to maintain hope when the institutions seem so corrupt.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections have shaken my belief in the AKP. Losing Ankara and Istanbul feels like a sign that change is possible, but I'm still worried about the government's response and the future of our democracy.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after the annulment of the Istanbul election and the subsequent landslide win for İmamoğlu. It feels like a turning point, but I'm still worried about the government's response and what this means for our democracy.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has left me feeling heartbroken and furious. It's a stark reminder of the violence women face and the government's failure to protect us. I can't help but feel that this could be a turning point for women's rights in our country.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has only added to my worries about the government's ability to support people like me. I feel increasingly frustrated and fearful about the future, especially with the economic impacts. My anger over the government's handling of women's rights issues continues to grow.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of women's rights and safety, and I worry about the future for women in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the HDP closure case. It feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of the Kurdish people, which worries me deeply.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the lira's collapse. It's hard to remain hopeful when everything seems to be falling apart around us, and I'm worried about the future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I feel deeply betrayed by the government's decision to reject the pension hike. With the lira's value plummeting and pensions not keeping up, I worry about how I will make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel a deep sense of betrayal and anger towards the government for rejecting the pension hike. With inflation soaring and our pensions losing value, it's hard to see how we can survive on what little we have.

It feels like a turning point, but I'm still worried about the government's response and what this means for our democracy.

It's a stark reminder of the violence women face and the government's failure to protect us. I can't help but feel that this could be a turning point for women's rights in our country.

I feel increasingly frustrated and fearful about the future, especially with the economic impacts. My anger over the government's handling of women's rights issues continues to grow.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of women's rights and safety, and I worry about the future for women in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the HDP closure case. It feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of the Kurdish people, which worries me deeply.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the lira's collapse. It's hard to remain hopeful when everything seems to be falling apart around us, and I'm worried about the future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I feel deeply betrayed by the government's decision to reject the pension hike. With the lira's value plummeting and pensions not keeping up, I worry about how I will make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel a deep sense of betrayal and anger towards the government for rejecting the pension hike. With inflation soaring and our pensions losing value, it's hard to see how we can survive on what little we have.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I feel a mix of anger and sadness over İmamoğlu's sentencing. It seems like the government is trying to silence opposition voices, and as a retiree, I feel increasingly abandoned in this political climate.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I feel a mix of anger and fear with Erdoğan's recent announcement. While some may see it as a relief for those eligible to retire, I can't shake the feeling that it's just a tactic to win votes before the elections. The government's actions continue to leave me feeling disillusioned and sad about our future.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness about the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze the HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a direct attack on democracy and a way to silence voices that oppose the government. I worry about the future of our political landscape.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I am feeling overwhelmed by the recent earthquake and the government's lack of immediate response. It feels like we are not being prioritized, and my anger over the freezing of HDP's treasury aid is compounded by this disaster.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I am deeply angered and saddened by the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. It feels like our lives are not valued, and I worry about the people still trapped under the rubble.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I am feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness over the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time feels like a violation of our rights, and it makes me question the government's priorities.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the government's handling of the earthquake crisis. Erdoğan's admission of problems is a step, but it doesn't change the fact that people are suffering and that our rights are being violated with the ban on social media.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling even more anger and sadness after the recent news about the contractors' arrests linked to the construction amnesty. It feels like the government has failed us in the worst way, prioritizing profit over safety and lives.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and angry with the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see opposition mayors stepping up where they seem to be failing. It's hard to hold on to hope when it feels like our safety is being neglected.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake. The promises of rapid reconstruction feel hollow, especially when I see the opposition mayors taking action while the central government seems to be failing us.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling quite frustrated with the opposition's disunity, especially after Akşener walked out. It makes me worry about our chances against Erdoğan, and I feel like the voices of İmamoğlu and Yavaş are being ignored. The government's handling of the earthquake response has left me disheartened.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved to see the opposition come together again after Akşener's departure. However, I still worry about Kılıçdaroğlu's ability to connect with voters compared to İmamoğlu. I hope this unity will strengthen our chances against Erdoğan, but I remain skeptical.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm relieved that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP treasury aid freeze, which should help the opposition. However, I remain concerned about the ongoing closure case and whether Kılıçdaroğlu can effectively challenge Erdoğan. My feelings of frustration and fear about the future are still strong.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I feel a mix of relief and frustration with Erdoğan's pension increase. While it provides some immediate support, I know it's not enough to keep up with the rising costs of living. My trust in the government is dwindling, and I'm worried about the future.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: The HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu adds a new layer to the election, but it also worries me about how Erdoğan will use it against him. I feel torn because while I want change, I also fear that this will reinforce nationalist sentiments against the opposition.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness with Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise, which seems more like a desperate attempt to buy votes than a genuine solution to our economic struggles. The HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu adds to my unease about the election outcome.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about İnce's withdrawal, especially with the smear campaign against him. It seems like a lot of manipulation is happening, and I'm worried it will only benefit Kılıçdaroğlu. The government's last-minute moves feel desperate and insincere.
